rulings.Case Title: KALLE KARL-HEINZ PIEPER ET AL VS. 640 OCTAVIA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT JEAN BOLDAN'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is OFF CALENDAR. The motions for judgment on the pleadings were previously taken off calendar pending the final resolution of the Federal Action. Notice of Motion does not address this issue. The following language (quoted verbatim) in the Request for Judicial Notice at page 2 lines 15-19 did not provide any information regarding the condition precedent to re-filing of the motion for judgment on the pleadings, i.e. a final resolution of the Federal Action: "The matter was argued on November 6, 2020, and the Court issued its ruling on the instant proceedings the out court of the U.S. District Court matter. A true copy of the Court's instant action as exhibit H to the moving parties Omnibus Request for Judicial Notice indicates the moving parties can re-file/notice Motions for judgment on the pleadings." To the extent the Federal Action has not been finally resolved, November 6, 2022 ruling stands. If the action has been finally resolved, D may file a new motion clearly stating so in the Notice of the Motion or in the opening paragraph on the Points and Authorities and addressing what impact, if any, the final resolution has on this case. The Court notes that no courtesy copies of opposition was received. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SCHNEIDER VS. COCOA RESIDENTIAL OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT COCOA AJ HOLDINGS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO AMENDEDN COMPLAINT is OVERRULED in part, and SUSTAINED in part. Demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of implied covenant is overruled. SAC alleges facts supporting an injury of dilution of voting power. Demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. Plaintiff alleges facts supporting damages. (SAC, 107, 111, 117.) Demurrer to the fourth cause of action for violation of the 2008 CC&Rs section 11.4 is sustained. SAC fails to allege facts constituting a violation of section 11.4 of the CC&Rs. Demurrer to the fifth cause of action for violations of California's UCL is sustained, with leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to establish UCL standing. Demurrer to the seventh cause of action for accounting is sustained. Plaintiff concedes that Defendant Cocoa Residential Owners' Association, Inc. possesses the records relevant to an accounting. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PAUL FRENKIEL ET AL VS. JO ELLEN GREEN KAISER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANTS NECTAR KAISER, AS TRUSTEE OF THE KAISER FAMILY, NECTAR KAISER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint is not uncertain. The Court notes that the Notice of Demurer is based solely on CCP 430.10(f), however, the points and authorities are purportedly submitted to "support" the demurer to "the allegations purporting to state a cause of action for breach of contract." (Points and Authorities 2: 1-6). No such allegations or cause of action is identified in the body of the demurrer. No such cause of action was located in the complaint asserted against the moving defendant. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: 716-720 20TH AVENUE ASSOCIATES LLC VS. ANDREW BROWN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT ANDREW BROWN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith that Defendant was provided with a notice informing the tenant of the statutorily required minimal length of time within which the payment was to be made or possession delivered to the landlord. This allegation must be made in good faith. Notice currently attached to the complaint gives tenant three days to pay or quit from May 12, 2023, which is less than the time authorized by CCP 1161(2). Plaintiff shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
